LHX.sentence: L3Harris dropped about 1% today and is looking expensive compared to the other big defense contractors, but defense spending keeps growing steadily.

ASML.sentence: ASML jumped over 6% today, which has pushed it to pretty expensive levels — but they're still the only company in the world that makes the machines needed for the most advanced computer chips.

LIN.sentence: Linde is having a quiet day with industrial gas demand steady, and they're locked into some huge multi-billion dollar contracts supplying the new chip factories being built in Ohio, Arizona, and Texas.

MSFT.sentence: Microsoft is basically flat — the big test will be whether Azure cloud growth accelerates above 30% in the next earnings, which would confirm the AI story is really paying off.

TMO.sentence: Thermo Fisher is down about 1% but looks like good value here — their bioprocessing business hit bottom and should start recovering over the next year as biotech companies ramp up again.

ENB.sentence: Enbridge is holding steady, though the pipeline company is looking a bit expensive compared to Treasury bonds right now.

NOW.sentence: ServiceNow is down about 1% and has fallen over 50% from its high, but corporate customers keep signing up for their AI workflow tools.

GLNCY.sentence: Glencore dropped 2% today as commodity prices stay choppy, but they make money both from mining and trading, so volatility can actually help them.

IBIT.sentence: Bitcoin is up slightly and sitting about 15% below its recent highs, which after a big run-up is actually pretty stable behavior.

KOF.sentence: Coca-Cola FEMSA jumped almost 3% today, helped by the Mexican peso staying strong against the dollar.

PBR.A.sentence: Petrobras is down about 2% even though oil prices are strong above $100, which is a bit puzzling for a company that usually moves with oil.

AMKBY.sentence: Maersk is up about 1% and trading below what it would cost to replace their fleet of container ships, which suggests the market thinks shipping is cheap right now.
